CDLE Unemployment Insurance — Agent Training Guide

Internal Use Only — Customer Service Representatives

Overview

This guide covers the most frequent unemployment insurance questions handled by CDLE phone agents. Use these answers as your baseline. When a situation isn't covered here, consult the policy manual or escalate to your supervisor.

Monetary Eligibility — What Wages Are Enough?

Claimants need enough wages in their base period (first four of the last five completed calendar quarters) to qualify. Here's what to tell them:

  • Total base period wages must be at least $2,500
  • Their highest-earning quarter must show at least $1,800
  • Total wages must be at least 1.25 times their high-quarter wages

If they don't meet monetary eligibility, inform them of the right to appeal and provide the appeal instructions.

Weekly Benefit Amount (WBA)

WBA is 60% of average weekly wages across the two highest base period quarters. Key caps:

  • Minimum WBA: $25
  • Maximum WBA: $700 per week

If the claimant asks why their benefit seems low, walk them through the base period wage calculation. Do not promise a specific amount — the system calculates it.

Duration of Benefits

Benefits last between 8 and 26 weeks depending on wage history. Extended Benefits (EB) may add up to 13 weeks during periods of high unemployment (state IU rate above 6%).

Work Search

Claimants must conduct an active job search weekly. The requirement is:

  • At least 3 employer contacts per week (at least 2 direct applications)
  • Logged in MyUI+ by midnight Sunday of the claimed week
  • Must apply for positions they are qualified and available to accept

If a claimant refuses a suitable job offer without good cause, they are disqualified for 8 weeks.

Weekly Certification

Remind claimants they must certify each week that they:

  1. Were able and available to work
  2. Met the work search requirement
  3. Did not refuse suitable work
  4. Reported any earnings (amounts above $25 or 25% of WBA, whichever is greater, are deducted)

Disqualifying Separations

Benefits are denied when separation from work was due to:

  • Voluntary quit without good cause attributable to the employer
  • Discharge for misconduct connected to the work
  • Fraud in connection with a claim

Disqualification lasts until the claimant earns at least $1,500 in new employment after disqualification.

Filing Deadline

Claims should be filed within 2 weeks of becoming unemployed. Late claims require a good cause showing — this is at the adjudicator's discretion.

Age Requirements

Claimants must be at least 18 years old to file for unemployment benefits.

If a caller under 18 asks about eligibility, explain they do not qualify and direct them to youth employment resources.

Benefit Year

Once a claim is active, the claimant has a 52-week benefit year beginning the Sunday of the week their initial claim was filed.

Escalation

Escalate to a senior agent or supervisor if:

  • The caller is disputing an adjudication decision
  • The caller reports suspected identity theft on their account
  • A fraud alert is active on the account
  • The caller needs an interpreter and the queue wait is over 10 minutes

Do not make commitments about eligibility outcomes. Use the phrases: "Based on what you've described, it sounds like you may be eligible" and "The final determination will be made by an adjudicator."

CRM Logging

Log all calls within 5 minutes of completion. Record: caller name, claim ID, topic, action taken, and any follow-up needed.
